Phew 😅

If it's an inside day, trading +2 or -2 back to vwap is a good strategy. But you never want to enter off vwap on these inside days, because that's where price chops. Now, if it's an outside day, you can't use that strategy... instead you can try an entry from vwap and sometimes ride it all the way to the 3's.

How do you get the bands with vwap
Great vid Andrea, really interesting stuff.

I use bands in my trading in combination with overnight volume profile. Bands are great because the markets are ranging between 70% and 80% of the time. Trends are not common. I’m great at trading the range with bands. But I’m still refining my strategy for determining when the trend will continue or there will be a breakout. And for whatever reason, this way of trading is not as popular so solid content is harder to come by. This was a great video and great explanation for those who may not fully understand the reasoning behind VWAP and standard deviations. Like you said, the market is all about finding balance after extended periods of price being at a discount or premium.
